app_caption: **Streamlit v1.56.0** new feature `st.iframe` demo — Fuji Speedway / GR Supra / RM Tyre / Dry conditions

sidebar_data_folder_help: Enter the path to the folder containing CSV files.
sidebar_session_detail:
**Session Details**
- Circuit: `{circuit}`
- Car: `{car}`
- Tyre: `{tyre}` / `{condition}`

error_no_show_fn:
`{version}/app.py` does not define a `show()` function.

Each version's `app.py` must expose `def show():`.
tab1_api_desc:
Pass an **HTML string** directly to `st.iframe` to render it as inline HTML.

```python
st.iframe(
    html_string,     # str — pass the HTML string directly
    height="content" # 'content' auto-adjusts to content height
)
```

| Parameter | Options |
|---|---|
| `height` | `"content"` / `"stretch"` / `int(px)` |
| `width` | `"stretch"` / `"content"` / `int(px)` |
| `tab_index` | `None` / `-1` / `int` |

> With `height="content"`, Streamlit measures the actual HTML height  
> and auto-resizes the iframe.
tab2_api_desc:
Pass a **`Path` object** to `st.iframe` and Streamlit loads and embeds the file automatically.

```python
from pathlib import Path
st.iframe(
    Path("reports/telemetry.html"),
    height=800,
)
```

- `.html` / `.htm` files are embedded as-is
- PDF / images / SVG are served via Streamlit's media storage and rendered directly by the browser
- The file can be replaced on each run (here it is regenerated on lap selection change)
tab3_desc:
Pass an **absolute URL** to `st.iframe` to display that webpage inside the iframe.  
Here we embed **OpenStreetMap** showing Fuji Speedway (iframe embedding allowed).

```python
st.iframe(
    "https://www.openstreetmap.org/export/embed.html"
    "?bbox=138.9066%2C35.3659%2C138.9468%2C35.3835"
    "&layer=mapnik&marker=35.3748%2C138.9267",
    height=600,
)
```

> **Note**: For external URLs, cross-origin restrictions prevent  
> `height="content"` from working — it falls back to 400px.  
> Specify height as an integer `(px)` or `"stretch"`.
>
> ※ Sites like `docs.streamlit.io` block embedding via `X-Frame-Options`.  
> Choose services that allow iframe embedding.
tab4_api_desc:
`st.menu_button` is a widget that **opens a dropdown on click**.  
Like `st.button`, it returns the selected option and resets to `None` on the next rerun.

```python
action = st.menu_button(
    label="Export",
    options=["CSV", "JSON", "HTML"],
    type="primary",               # "primary" / "secondary" / "tertiary"
    icon=":material/download:",   # Material icon or emoji
    width="content",              # "content" / "stretch" / int(px)
    format_func=lambda x: x,     # display name mapper
    disabled=False,
    help="Tooltip",
)
if action == "CSV":
    ...  # handle selection
```

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `type` | Button style (primary/secondary/tertiary) |
| `icon` | Material icon or emoji |
| `width` | Controls button width |
| `format_func` | Map option values to display names |
| `disabled` | Disable the button |
| `on_click` | Callback on selection |

> Unlike `st.selectbox`, the button label does not change —  
> the return value resets to `None` on the next rerun.
tab5_api_desc:
Three major new features added to `st.dataframe` are demonstrated here.

```python
event = st.dataframe(
    df,
    on_select="rerun",
    # ① Always keep exactly one row selected
    selection_mode="single-row-required",
    # ② Programmatically set the initial selected row
    selection_default={"selection": {"rows": [0], "columns": [], "cells": []}},  # 0-based index
    column_config={
        "col_a": st.column_config.NumberColumn(
            # ③ Control text alignment per column
            alignment="right",  # "left" / "center" / "right"
        ),
    },
    hide_index=True,
)
# Retrieve selected row indices
rows = event.selection.rows  # [0]
```

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `selection_mode` | `"single-row-required"` keeps exactly one row selected at all times |
| `selection_default` | `{"selection": {"rows": [N], ...}}` sets the initial selected row programmatically |
| `alignment` | `"left"` / `"center"` / `"right"` controls text alignment within a column |

> **Key point**: The lap selected in the sidebar is synced  
> to the initial selected row in the table.  
> Click any row to switch selection and update the chart.
tab5_hint: Click a row to update the chart (initial row synced to sidebar lap selection)
